pattern: Once you know the single largest value in the whole array, a sliding window can track how many times it currently appears, and shrinking from the left the moment that count is enough tells you, for every right edge, how many valid left edges exist.
hints: The value you are counting occurrences of is fixed for the entire array -- find the overall maximum once, up front, before thinking about any subarray.
Grow a window from the left as you scan each right edge, keeping a running count of how many times the overall maximum appears inside the current window.

The moment the running count reaches the required amount, every position from the current left edge back to the start of the array also works as a left edge for this right edge (they only ever contain the same maximum occurrences or more), so shrink the window until the count drops below the requirement and add the number of positions you shrank past to your running total.

edge_inputs.description: repeated max value scattered through a mixed array
max value appears fewer times than required
single element array with the smallest valid k
the maximum occurs far fewer times overall than k, so no subarray qualifies
the maximum only occurs at the two ends of the array
k equal to the exact total number of times the maximum occurs
k equal to the array length, only satisfied when every element is the maximum

stress.description: large array from a restricted alphabet so the maximum repeats often, with a random threshold
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    values_pool = max(1, n // 20)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, values_pool) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: a small alphabet relative to length keeps the maximum's occurrence count high and interesting
